title: Binder Hub: Crosstalk-Coupled Line Vectoring
strategies: uniform power split, index decode order (do-nothing baseline)
single-shot independent water-filling ignoring the coupling matrix entirely, index decode order (the obvious first approach; textbook single-user water-filling)

probe the interference graph to rank lines by how much crosstalk they impose on others, choose a cancellation-friendly decode order from that probe, then run interference-aware iterative water-filling (alternate: recompute each line's effective noise from currently-uncancelled crosstalk under the chosen order, re-water-fill against that effective noise, repeat to a fixed point)

compare several probe-driven orders (static out-degree sort vs. greedy peeling on the live interference graph) plus the coupling-free fallback, keep whichever converged allocation achieves the highest true sum-rate
